title: The Owl and the Pussy-Cat
author: Edward Lear
source: Project Gutenberg eBook #13650 (Nonsense Songs, Stories, Botany, and Alphabets)
note: Three-stanza nonsense poem, one stanza per spread. Internal phrase repetition ('You are, You are, You are!') pulls TTR down despite Lear's invented vocabulary (Piggy-wig, bong-tree, runcible). Will exercise the prosody module when it lands — anapestic feet, ABABCC-leaning scheme.
